Neo-Calvinism is a [[hydroplane]] with a belief in the unknowableness of salvation, and the impossibility to change your outcome. Instead of imbuing humility, it imbues a narcissistic overcompensation. Since people are not able to work to be saved the alternative is to overcompensate to convince themselves they are already saved, often this includes degrading others as “not-saved”. It is a prominent heresy of Evangelicals today, and it is linked to Romantic Nationalism which often says the nation or its ingroup is saved and others are not. The solution is faith in the achievability of salvation through believing in and following the firmlaw, and developing a quiet but firm confidence in salvation from your practice<ref>https://evolution.faith/hydroplaning/</ref>
== References ==

[[Category:Hydroplanes]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Neo-Calvinism]][[sn:Neo-Calvinism]]
[[Category:Pages]]
